Notes / Context: Large spa hotel with flat rooftop. High energy costs from pool heating & spa operations. Interested in sustainability branding.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Good industry fit, large roof area, high energy consumer, owns property, in NordLight service area.

Notes / Context: Boutique luxury hotel in Art Nouveau villa. Looking to reduce operating costs and appeal to eco-conscious tourists.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Property owner, premium brand that values sustainability, in Pärnu coverage area. Heritage building may need permit review.

Notes / Context: Largest Estonian-owned 3PL provider. Multiple large warehouse roofs. Looking into ESG reporting & energy cost reduction.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Large flat warehouse roofs ideal for solar. High electricity usage. Owns facilities. Strong budget signals.

Notes / Context: Private daycare chain with multiple locations. Laagri building has good south-facing roof. Interested in green marketing to parents.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Property owner, good roof orientation, sustainability is a selling point to customers, municipal subsidies may apply.

Notes / Context: Largest organic dairy producer in Estonia. Modern production facility with large roof. Already has sustainability certifications (FSSC 22000, ISO 45001).
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Large energy consumer, owns facility, sustainability-oriented brand. Saaremaa is outside core area but reachable.

Notes / Context: Couple in their 40s, renovated 1930s wooden house. South-facing roof, no shading. Interested in reducing €180/month electricity bill.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Homeowner, good roof, motivated by cost savings, in Tallinn, came through paid advertising (high intent).

Notes / Context: Mid-range city-center hotel. Looking at energy efficiency upgrades as part of planned renovation in 2027.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Good timing signal (renovation planned), property owner, in Tartu service area. Longer sales cycle but solid opportunity.

Notes / Context: Artisan bakery with own building. Ovens run 14hrs/day — very high electricity costs. Owner is personally passionate about sustainability.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Property owner, extremely high energy usage for size, sustainability-motivated, in Tallinn.

Notes / Context: Trendy croissanterie in rented space in Tartu city center. Interested in 'going green' but doesn't own the property.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Tenant, not property owner — would need landlord approval. Small scale. Green interest is genuine but may not have decision power over building.

Notes / Context: Small food producer on Hiiumaa island. Owns building. Interested in energy independence due to unreliable grid on island.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Owns property, motivated by energy independence. But Hiiumaa is outside core service area — logistics of installation would be expensive. Small deal size.

Notes / Context: Freight forwarding company — office-only operation, no warehouses owned. Rents 200m² office space in business park.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Tenant, no suitable roof space. Office-only business with low energy consumption. No property ownership.

Notes / Context: Lives in 9th floor apartment in Soviet-era panel building. Interested in solar but has no roof access or decision authority.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Apartment dweller — no roof ownership or access. Cannot install solar panels. Building decisions require HOA approval from 100+ owners.

Notes / Context: Major tech company inquiring about solar for their HQ. Enterprise-level procurement process, 12+ month decision cycle.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Way too large for NordLight — enterprise company with complex procurement. Would need a much larger solar provider. Outside NordLight's typical deal size and capability.

Notes / Context: Historic café on Tartu Town Hall Square. Operates in rented ground-floor space of a heritage-protected building.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Tenant in heritage-protected building. Cannot modify roof. Landlord has no interest in solar installation.

Notes / Context: Homeowner in Narva (eastern Estonia). Owns detached house with good roof. Interested but NordLight doesn't operate in Ida-Virumaa.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Outside NordLight's geographic service area (Narva/Ida-Virumaa). Would need to refer to local installer or expand operations.

Notes / Context: Private kindergarten in rented residential building in central Tallinn. Building owned by separate property company.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Tenant — doesn't own building. Even though industry fit is good, no authority over roof. Property owner has declined solar in the past.

Notes / Context: 3-person IT consulting firm. Works remotely from co-working spaces. No physical office or property. Curious about solar for personal home.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: No commercial property. Micro-company with no physical footprint. Personal interest should be re-routed as residential lead if they own a home.

Notes / Context: Small seasonal guesthouse on Hiiumaa. Open only June–August. Very small electricity usage. Owns property but tiny building.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Seasonal business (3 months), very low energy consumption, Hiiumaa is outside service area, tiny deal size (~€3K) not worth logistics cost.

Notes / Context: Distribution terminal in Tartu. Large flat-roof warehouse. However, building is leased from Mainor Ülemiste — not owned.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Great building for solar but tenant doesn't own the property. Decision authority lies with Mainor property management.

Notes / Context: Young family, just built new house in Viimsi. Roof is north-facing with heavy tree cover. Already spent full renovation budget.
Why Qualifies / Disqualifies: Poor roof orientation (north-facing), significant shading from trees, no available budget after construction. Property is suitable in theory but practical constraints make it unviable.
